It is strange to me how all of you hype this up when not too many years ago it was seen as a scam and I'm just confused. What am I talking about? Well remember how back in the day it was a HUGE deal that ASUS released a NATIVE 240Hz monitor? The first of its kind and not like the other brands that had to (at the time) FAKE it by generating frames in between to make it look like 165Hz etc? I know the tech has gotten better, but it's funny to me how gamers used to celebrate native performance and look down on fake generation and demanded better, now we're celebrating that very thing.

Turn off all the AI tech and compare the cards that way and let yourself realise you're paying multiple month salaries for that miniscule improvement, you aren't paying for the card, you're paying for the AI at this point, an AI to fool you into thinking the card is more powerful than it is. Again it LOOKS cool and might even FEEL cool just walking around and chill gaming, but how responsive and smooth is it really during extreme high intensity stuff most of you will do? Or are you really planning on buying this card for walking around in gaming? Even as a single player old chill man at this point I have to question this.

If you prefer it, cool, genuinely, that's yoyr opinion and it's awesome, I just cannot justify the pricing and the idea of quite literally paying to be fooled that you have a strong PC by an AI, it's werid to me.

I was with my best friend minutes before he died. I had never seen cancer take a life before, the hospital has pamphlets about death but it is not the same. the body was like a shell of him. it was not scary. I was scared to touch him though because I didnt know if the cancer hurt his skin or my touch would be painful. I felt sad to see him like that but so grateful to have as much time as I could with him. I kissed his forehead and it was cold. like his soul was already released. I just wanted to stay with him in that room holding his hand forever. the hardest part was knowing I would never see him again. rest in peace Kerry. I once told him, "Kerry, you are such a gentleman, a real old fashioned gentleman and that so nice to see these days.." he leaned over and with a twinkle in his eye he replied softly," I have my mother to thank for that!"

In TypeScript, if you declare an array as readonly, you can't use the push method to add items. The readonly keyword stops the array from being changed. Instead, use the spread operator (...) to make a new array with the new items. For example: TypeScript let arr: readonly number[] = [1, 2, 3]; arr = [...arr, 4, 5]; // creates a new array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] This makes a new array, without changing the old one.

This solution seems to cause repeated DFS paths for each crs within each iteration of numCourses since visitSet is reset each time. In my implementation, I used "visited" to keep track of the path to avoid re-traversal.  I check the length of the value of the selected key/course first to know if we have checked it before. class Solution: def canFinish(self, numCourses: int, prerequisites: List[List[int]]) -> bool: adjList = { i:set() for i in range(numCourses)} visited = set() for a,b in prerequisites: adjList[a].add(b) def dfs(curr): if len(adjList[curr]) == 0: return True if curr in visited: return False visited.add(curr) tmp = adjList[curr].copy() for pre in tmp: if not dfs(pre): return False adjList[curr].remove(pre) return True for i in range(numCourses): if not dfs(i): return False return True

Thomas Sowell on Affirmative Action:
It [Affirmative action] put many Black students, with all the prerequisites for success, into places where they were almost guaranteed to fail. I'll go all the way back to 1965 when I was teaching at Cornell. They suddenly brought in large numbers of Black students under affirmative action. And in an ultimately short time, half of them were on academic probation for academic deficiencies. And so I went over to the administration building and looked up their SAT scores. The average Black student at Cornell at that time was at the 75th percentile, better than three-quarters of other American students who took the SAT, but the average student in the Cornell Liberal Arts College was at the 99th percentile. These black students were people who could have [succeeded] someplace else. Had they gone to a college they qualified for, they would have been in the top of their class, but at Cornell they continually failed. So in the end, you had the great bulk of those Black students, an absolute majority, fail to graduate. And there's no great gain from flunking out of an elite institution."

I'm Danish, we are a great example of why socialism doesn't work, why?
The Danish economy is capitalist with a social security network.
Despite the world's highest total tax pressure, a very efficient workforce and very low corruption,we can barely keep everything running.
That means that under something quite close to ideal circumstances, having an extensive social security program, is barely possible.
Now  remove the capitalist mechanics paying for all of this and introduce the economic inefficiencies inherint to socialism and you'll see the whole house of cards come crashing down very hard.
Social programs of this magnitude are only possible when you have an efficient and homogenous population willing to wave goodbye to way over 50% of their income in taxes (considering total tax pressure, ie income tax at minimum 32%(I pay 45%), sales tax at 25%, car sales tax at 200% etc).
Now, we Danes are generally ok with this, but just remember, it's a huge sacrifice, and it isn't even socialism.

The min-heap method surely has a time complexity of O((n-k)*log(n)). First, O(n) is needed for the heap's creation. Second, elements are removed until the heap has a size of k, which happens n-k times. Third, each removal operation is O(log(n)). Therefore, O((n-k)*log(n)) is the combined complexity, which is less efficient than the heap construction step.

Some years ago, I wrote something similar, but more connected with our current time of year (here in the USA) -- a snowfall simulation. Instead of having a single drop point, a (parameter controlled) number of 'snowflakes' were spawned at random points on the topmost line of pixels and then 'drifted' down (didn't go straight down, they had a chance to perturb the path to the left or right each time). Each had a random velocity (also parameterized for max). I also put a parameter for wind so all the snowflakes would tend to drift with the wind.  All the parameters were sliders so they could be changed while it was running. Snow would accumulate at the bottom and would fall down to the localize minimum point to the left or right. It was kind of fun to write and watch.

Chap is a bit long winded - are his power points the same?  But some great points are made. 
I find three basic categories serve to aid focus: start a conversation, wrap one up, or incidents.  The first two are a get in, get it, and get out proposition.  Pace these briskly.  Examples of the first might be investment strategies, the second the performance of those [quarterly or what have you].  Advertising campaigns behave in similar fashion. Incidents are problem solving territory: something new is forcing an adjustment or something went wrong and remediation needs attention.  Going granular is unavoidable, but the goal is engagement above all: the other people gathered are going to be part of the solution, and are not simple sponges. Time limit yourself to leave breathing room for the back end, because there's only one smartest guy in the room, and I'm not it!
Personally, I like to avoid superfluous text content: some things need pictures, others don't.  For investing... charts present trends quickly and solidly in a way that simple adjectves sometimes find trouble.   There's just a touch of vaudville in presentations: audience feedback is immediate in an unspoken way: they're either captured or not - learning this [pace] is done by doing: it has to be you, it has to be live. The key principles: 
1. Message: Try to understand the information by the context, you learn for real when you build the meaning of the words. That's exactly what we do when we are learning our mother tongue.
2. Importance: Make the target language important. The best way to do that is choosing the target language to do some important daily activities inspite of using your native language.
3. Observation: Listen more and more and more, as much as you can in order to perceive the vocabulary and the way the natives speak the language.
4. Comprehension: The more you comprehend, the more you learn. Don't worry about acumulating words, but comprehending them.

There is also the free edition of BBEdit (formerly TextWrangler) which looks a bit spartan but has a lot of the features most people would expect. I use it mostly for quick-and-dirty text treatments like stripping text of rich formatting and sorting long lists.

But for now, Brackets fits the bill, even though I have access to Dreamweaver which is controversial as I understand, but has come a long way from the good ol’ WYSIWYG days.

Shorting the COM is not a cheap circuit board design necessarily. The cheap boards will actually just have them shorted on a ground plane or something or to the battery like you have.. Its done disconnected  that way so you can have an isolated load. The relay might be switching something that is at a different voltage or even a different common mode entirely.

Suppose instead of one battery, you had like a 5V line controlling the logic and a 24V battery or something, by isolating them you can use both without them shorting. On this board that has the 18V from your batteries shorted to the relay COM you do not have an option to use a seperate battery. 

Suppose also that you were not connecting a battery to the load, or it was an AC load. There are plenty of cases where the load is not at the full battery voltage and you would want them isolated.

I definitely think Hillary Clinton's viewpoint that the American people care about details, analyses, facts, and legislative precision was inaccurate. A large portion of both the primaries and the general election *is* a personality contest, whether we like to admit it or not. You need to learn to appeal to different crowds while still maintaining a focus on your agenda items.

It wasn't that Hillary didn't have that focus - I think she expended far too much energy in maintaining it, and not enough on figuring out what crowds wanted, or how to tailor her speeches so that both laypeople and those in-the-know would get a sense of who she was as a person and leader.

The end result of the loss can be blamed on the timing of a variety of events and the presence of a variety of scandals, real or imagined, but a large part of the responsibility lies with how she chose to run a data-centric, cold-and-clinical campaign. I hope future leaders learn from her missteps.

As for her commentary on Obama and her husband...I don't think they ran anywhere near as clinically as she believes. I do think they were more capable of being a visionary, of matching personalities with their opponents.

The energy saved by leaving the car in gear will be more than the energy required to get over that next hill, or the coasting car would not be able to make it. I do the following with a manual transmission. If I need the car to continue rolling and there is no stop or speed limit I coast by pressing the clutch. If I need to stop or respect the speed limit I use the engine break as much as possible before using the breaks. To really save fuel using engine break the driver needs to think always what comes ahead. Most modern cars with automatic transmission and CVT also use a mild engine break when lightly pressing the breaks but the driver needs to anticipate stops and be pacient. In a manual transmission, engine break in a lower gear is also very nice before turning at high speed.

I would love to see an even higher-level video that situates Machine Learning / Neural Networks within the panoply of statistical techniques for data reduction and feature extraction/recognition.

For example, PCA and SVD represent the apex of a long line of statistical techniques that do their thing without training, that answer the question What can this data tell me about itself?

Machine Learning is at the other extreme, requiring not only extensive training, but also the separation of data into training and validation sets, answering the question How does this data relate to all other data I've seen?  And Machine Learning itself is split into many sub-domains, primarily based on the presence or absence of tagged data to use for learning, but also on the need for time-domain processing (versus single-event processing).

In between fall low training techniques, such as Gradient Boosting, and perhaps some Bayesian techniques.

How do we know that the A filters, B filters and C filters are really oriented at 0°, 22.5° and 45° respectively unless they've been calibrated with each other beforehand? The shooter had no criminal record and was cleared of all background checks.  He purchased most(or all) of the guns legally.  Tell me, how could you have predicted that this one person would've committed the worst mass shooting in the US to date? The fact that he was able to get his hands on SO many weapons without a blip in the system goes to show the weakness in our system.  Yes, people kill people but guns facilitate the killing. A valid reason to have a single or limited firearm would be for instance, running a jewelry store. But the fact that this scum of the earth got his hands on so many guns without raising a red flag is the issue here.  If he had just a single handgun, there would be far fewer casualties.  

If other countries are able to implement effective gun control, eliminating future mass murder cases, why can't we? 

The second point that Franco notes is also very important - the media is glorifying these killers. There should be laws for these criminals to remain unnamed and to focus solely on the victims.  People who commit crimes like these want the attention in their own very sick ways but we should wipe their existence from our minds and memories asap.

One of Hillary's problems is that her positions on issues may have seemed more grounded, by they also didn't solve the problem. For example: When Sanders talked about public college being free (through a regulatory measure, similar to what is done in the UK), Hillary's proposal was tuition free college in which tax credits would be given to help subsidize the cost of college, but only for families below a certain income bracket. So you have tax money being used for college credits for certain people, which does nothing to actually curb the out-of-control costs of college education. All it does is give colleges an excuse to increase their tuition rates because now, the money would be coming from the government instead of students. \n\nAnother example: Universal Healthcare. Hillary said, 90% of people are covered, so it's easier to just get the other 10%. Maybe. But the 90% don't all have good, or affordable coverage. They're just covered. The plan of extending Obamacare further doesn't do anything to actually curb the out-of-control costs of healthcare, because her plan continued to see cooperation with the money-sucking insurance companies. See? There's a pattern here.

ನನ್ನ ತಾಯಿ ಆರೋಗ್ಯದಿಂದಲೇ ಇದ್ದರು, 6 ತಿಂಗಳ ಹಿಂದೆ ದೊಡ್ಡ ಸೊಸೆ ಏಕಾಏಕಿ ಬಂದು ನನ್ನ ತಾಯಿಯ ಎದೆಗೆ ಹೊಡೆದಿದ್ದರು,ಶ್ವಾಸಕೋಶದಲ್ಲಿ ರಕ್ತ ಹೆಪ್ಪುಗಟ್ಟಿ ಆಚೆಗೂ ಬರದೆ ಒಳಗೇ ಇನ್ಫೆಕ್ಷನ್ ಆಗಿ ಉಸಿರಾಟದ ತೊಂದರೆಯಾಗಿ ಸುಸ್ತು ಆಗಿ ಇರುತ್ತಿದ್ದರು,ಆಸ್ಪತ್ತೆಗಳಲ್ಲಿ ತೋರಿಸಿ ಚಿಕಿತ್ಸೆ ಕೊಡಿಸಿದೆವು, ಡಾಕ್ಟರ್ ಗ್ಯಾರಂಟಿ ಕೊಡಲಿಲ್ಲ, ಅಂದು 27.9.2024 ರ ಸಂಜೆ ನಮ್ಮೂರಿನ ಮನೆಗೆ ಕರೆದುಕೊಂಡು ಬಂದೆವು, ಊಟವನ್ನುಮಾಡಿದರು, ಮಾತನಾಡಿಕೊಂಡು ಇದ್ದೆವು, ಮಲಗುವುದಕ್ಕೆ ಹಾಸಿಗೆಯನ್ನು ಹಾಕಿಕೊಟ್ಟೆವು ಮಲಗಿಕೊಂಡು ಕೆಮ್ಮುತ್ತಾ ನಿದ್ರೆ ಮಾಡಿದರು, ನಾವೂ ನಿದ್ರೆ ಮಾಡಿದೆವು, ಒಂದು ಬಾರಿ ರಾತ್ರಿ 1.15 ರ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ ಎದ್ದು ಒಬ್ಬರೇ ಬಾಗಿಲು ತೆಗೆದು ಮನೆ ಮುಂದೆ ಹೋಗಿದ್ದರು, ತಕ್ಷಣ ನಾವು ಹೋಗಿ ಕೈ ಹಿಡಿದುಕೊಂಡು, ನಂತರ ಮನೆಗೆ ಕರೆದುಕೊಂಡು ಮಲಗಿಸಿದೆವು, ನಾವೂ ಮಲಗಿಕೊಂಡೆವು,ನಿದ್ರೆ ಮಾಡಲಿ ಎಂದು ಹೆಚ್ಚಾಗಿ ಮಾತನಾಡಿಸಲಿಲ್ಲ, ಆದರೆ ನಾವೆಲ್ಲರೂ ನಿದ್ರೆ ಮಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾಗ ಅಂದು 28.9.2024 ರ ಸುಮಾರು ಮುಂಜಾನೆ 3-4 ಗಂಟೆಗೆ ದೊಡ್ಡ ಉಸಿರು ಹೋಗಿದೆ, ಆದರೆ ನಾವು ನೋಡಿದ್ದು ಬೆಳಿಗ್ಗೆ 4 15 ರ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ, ಕುಳಿತುಕೊಂಡು ಶಬ್ದ ಮಾಡಿಕೊಂಡು ಕಣ್ಣು ಮುಚ್ಚಿದ್ದಾರೆ, ಉಸಿರಾಡುತ್ತಿದ್ದಾರೆ,ಆದರೆ ಮಾತನಾಡುತ್ತಿಲ್ಲ, 
 ಆಮೇಲೆ ಸಂಬಂಧಿಕರು ಮಾತನಾಡಿಸಿದಾಗ ಒಂದು ಬಾರಿ ಕಣ್ಣು ಬಿಟ್ಟು ನೋಡಿದರು, ಮತ್ತೆ ಕಣ್ಣುಮುಚ್ಚಿಕೊಂಡಡು ನಂತರ 3 ಬಾರಿ ಆಗಾಗ ಕಣ್ಣು ತೆರೆದು ನನ್ನನ್ನು ನೋಡಿದರು, ಅದೇ ಕಡೇ ನೋಟ, 
 ಹಾಲು ಕುಡಿಸಿದೆವು ಕುಡಿದರು, ಆಮೇಲೆ ಉಸಿರಾಟದ ಶಬ್ದ ಬರು ಬರುತ್ತಾ ಕಡಿಮೆಯಾಗುತ್ತಾ ಹೋಯಿತು, ಕಡೆಗೆ 7 ಗಂಟೆ 8 ನಿಮಿಷದ ಸಮಯದಲ್ಲಿ ತಾಯಿ ಕೊನೆಯುಸಿರೆಳೆದಿದ್ದಾರೆ ಒಂದು ಬಾರಿ ಉಸಿರನ್ನು ಜೋರಾಗಿ ಎಳೆದುಕೊಂಡಾಗ ಉಸಿರಾಟ ನಿಂತು ಹೋಯಿತು, ನನಗೆ ತುಂಬಾ ನೋವಾಯಿತು, ನಂಬೋಕೆ ಆಗಿಲ್ಲ, ಉಳಿಸಲು ಎಷ್ಟೋ ಪ್ರಯತ್ನ ಮಾಡಿದೆವು, ಜೀವ ಉಳಿಸಲು ಆಗಲಿಲ್ಲ, ಕೈಯಲ್ಲಿ ರಕ್ತನಾಡಿ ನಿಧಾನವಾಗಿ ಬಡಿದುಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತಿದೆ, ಕೈ ತಣ್ಣಗಾಗುತ್ತಿದೆ, ನನಗೆ ಭಯ ಆಗ್ತಿದೆ, ದೇವರೇ ಇನ್ಯಾರು ನನಗೆ ದಿಕ್ಕು ಅನಿಸುತ್ತಿದೆ, ಬಿಟ್ಟು ಒಂಟಿಯಾಗಿ ಹೋಗ್ತವ್ರಲ್ಲಾ, ಮನೆ ಬಿಟ್ಟು ಹೋಗ್ತವ್ರಲ್ಲಾ ಅಂತಾ ಮನಸಿಗೆ ವೇದನೆ ಆಯ್ತು, ದೊಡ್ಡ ಆಸ್ತಿ ಕಳೆದುಕೊಳ್ಳುತ್ತಿದ್ದೀನಲ್ಲಾ ಎಂದು ನೋವಾಗುತ್ತಿದೆ, , ನನ್ನ ಮೇಲೆ ಪ್ರಪಂಚವೇ ಬಿದ್ದಂಗಾಯ್ತು, ಇಷ್ಟು ಬೇಗ ಈ ರೀತಿ ಜೀವದ ಪ್ರಾಣ ಹೋಗುತ್ತೆ ಅಂತಾ ಯಾವತ್ತೂ ಅಂದುಕೊಂಡಿರಲಿಲ್ಲಾ..... ಪ್ರಪಂಚವೇ ಮುಳುಗಿ ಹೋದಂಗಾಯ್ತು, 
 ವಿಧಿ ಅನ್ನೋದು ದೊಡ್ಡದು, ಕ್ರೂರಿ, ಅದನ್ನು ಮೀರೋರು ಯಾರೂ ಇಲ್ಲಾ, ಇಂತಾ ಒಳ್ಳೆಯ ತಾಯಿಯನ್ನು ಯಮ ಬೇಗ ಕರಗದುಕೊಳ್ಳಬಾರದಿತ್ತು, ಇನ್ನೂ ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿ ನೋಡಿಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಅಟ್ ಲಾಸ್ಟ್ 1ದಿನವಾದರೂ ಉಳಿಸಬಹುದಾಗಿತ್ತು
  ವಾರ,ವರ್ಷವಾದರೂ ಜೀವ ಉಳಿಸಬಹುದಾಗಿತ್ತು, ಆಗಲೂ ಡಾಕ್ಟರ್ ಗೆ ಫೋನ್ ಮಾಡಿದೆನು ಆದರೆ ಯಾವುದೂ ಪ್ರಯೋಜನ ಆಗಲಿಲ್ಲ, 
 ಅದಕ್ಕೇ ಹೇಳೋದು ಜೀವ ಇದ್ದಾಗ ಅದಕ್ಕೆ ಬೆಲೆ ಕೊಡಿ ಸಂತೋಷದಿಂದಿರಿಸಿ, ಅದೇ ನಿಜವಾದ ಸಾಧನೆ. 
 ಆದರೆ ಒಳ್ಳೆ ರೀ‌ತಿಯಿಂದಲೇ ಪ್ರಾಣ ಹೋಗಿದೆ, 
 ಸ್ವರ್ಗ ಖಂಡಿತಾ ಸೇರಿ ಮತ್ತೆ ನಮ್ಮ ಮನೇಗೇ ಹುಟ್ಟಿ ಬರ್ತಾಳೆ,ಆಗ ಆ ಮಗುವನ್ನು ತಾಯಿ ಎಂದು ತಿಳಿದು ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿ ಸಂತೋಷದಿಂದ ನೋಡಿಕೊಂಡರೆ ನನ್ನ ಜೀವನ ಸಾರ್ಥಕ. 
 ಹಿಂದೆ 2 ದಿನದಲ್ಲಿ ನನಗೆ ಎಲ್ಲಾ ಬುದ್ದಿ ಮಾತನ್ನು ಹೇಳಿದ್ದಾಳೆ, ಅದನ್ನು ಮಾಡಲೇಬೇಕು, ಅವರ ಆಸೆಯನ್ನು ಈಡೇರಿಸಬೇಕು, ಬೇಗ ಮದುವೆಯಾಗಿ ಬರುವ ಸೊಸೆ ನನ್ನ ಹಾಗೆ ಛಾತಿಯಾಗಿ ಬದುಕಬೇಕು, ನಾಲ್ಕು ಜನರ ಮುಂದೆ ಒಳ್ಳೆಯ ಹೆಸರನ್ನು ತೆಗೆದು ಬದುಕಬೇಕು, ಎಲ್ಲಾ ಜವಾಬ್ದಾರಿಗಳನ್ನು ನೀನೇ ಮಾಡು, ಅವರ್ಯಾರಿಗೂ ಗೊತ್ತಿಲ್ಲ,ತಮ್ಮನನ್ನೂ ನೋಡಿಕೋ ಎಂದೆಲ್ಲಾ ಹೇಳಿದ್ದಾರೆ. 
 ನನಗೆ ಮೈಯೆಲ್ಲಾ ಜುಮ್ಮೆನಿಸುತ್ತಿದೆ ನೆನೆಸಿಕೊಂಡರೆ......... 
 ಈಗಲೂ ತಾಯಿಯ ಚಿಂತೆ ಕಾಡುತ್ತಿದೆ, ಅಮ್ಮ ಕಣ್ಮುಂದೆ ಇರಬೇಕಾಗಿತ್ತು ಅಂತಾ....... 
 ಆದರೂ ನಮ್ಮ ಬೆನ್ನ ಹಿಂದೆ ಇರ್ತಾರೆ, ಅವರ ಆಶೀರ್ವಾದ ಇದೆ, ಮುಂದೆ ಒಳ್ಳೇದಾಗುತ್ತೆ.
 ತಾಯಿಯೇ ನಿಜವಾದ ದೇವರು.
 ಗಟ್ಟಿ ಮುಟ್ಟಾಗಿದ್ದ ತಾಯಿ ಇಷ್ಟು ಬೇಗ ಈ ರೀತಿ ಆಗ್ತಾರಂತಾ ಗೊತ್ತಿರಲಿಲ್ಲ. ಆದರೂ ವಿಧಿ ಯಾವಾಗ ಆತ್ಮವನ್ನು ಕರೆದುಕೊಂಡು ಹೋಗುತ್ತಾ ಯಾರಿಗೂ ಗೊತ್ತಾಗಲ್ಲ,

Stripping away the math and science, think of the "nothing" before the universe not as the opposition of the something we know as the universe but as the ultimate state of potential. Once a "thing" comes into existence it's potential is immediately limited by how it relates and compares to something else. This makes nothing as we can grasp it being part of the universe a thing in its own right. A true nothing is a state of pure infinite potential and because of that the universe popping into existence is an inevitability. It is also impossible for us to understand what was before the universe because that perfect state is outside of existence.

As an electronics student, I am very glad to know that the government is focusing its policies on boosting the electronics industry. Semiconductors are often called the "modern gold," as many experts suggest that the countries leading in semiconductor technology will dominate the global economy. Therefore, it is important for India to set up more semiconductor plants to support both the electronics industry and the Indian economy.
 Over the past few decades, India has failed to engage and utilize the potential of its electronics students. This has not only led to brain drain but has also pushed many students to shift their careers toward other fields, such as software engineering or preparing for exams like GATE. I would be very happy if this plan is executed as the government claims.
 It is true that we are far behind semiconductor leaders like Taiwan, which manufactures chips at advanced nodes such as 2.5nm, while our upcoming plant will produce chips at the 28nm node. This shows that we still have a long journey ahead to go from 28nm to 2.5nm. (For context, the node refers to the smallest unit or feature size in a semiconductor chip.)
 Considering the scale of this industry, even ₹76,000 crore may seem small, but at least the government has taken the first step, which is a very satisfying start. The results of this initiative will be seen in the near future, and I remain hopeful for positive outcomes.

1941 ମସିହା ଜୁଲାଇ 16ରେ କଣିହାଁ ର ନାଲମ ଗାଁ ରେ ଜନ୍ମିତ ଜଣେ ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିବିଶେଷ ଏକ ସାଧାରଣ ଚାଷୀ ପରିବାରରୁ ଆସି ଓଡ଼ିଶା ରାଜନୀତିରେ ନିଜର ପରିଚୟ ସୃଷ୍ଟି କରିଥିବା ତଥା ସରଳ ଜୀପନଯାପନ ପାଇଁ ଯାହାଙ୍କ ପରିଚୟ ରହିଛି, ସେ ହେଉଛନ୍ତି ଆମ ରାଜ୍ୟର ପୂର୍ବତନ କେନ୍ଦ୍ରମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଡାକ୍ତର ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ପ୍ରଧାନ । 
 ଡା. ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ପ୍ରଧାନ 1980ରେ ବିଜେପିର ପ୍ରତିଷ୍ଠା ସମୟରୁ ନିଜର ରାଜନୈତିକ ଜୀବନ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲେ । ଦୀର୍ଘ 43 ବର୍ଷ ପୂର୍ବରୁ ନିଜର ଡାକ୍ତରୀ ପେଶାକୁ ପଛରେ ପକାଇ ସମାଜସେବା ପ୍ରତି ଜୀବନକୁ ସମର୍ପିତ କରିଥିଲେ । ଯେତେବେଳେ ଭାରତୀୟ ଜନତା ପାର୍ଟିରେ ଲୋକ ଆସିବା ପାଇଁ ଡରୁଥିଲେ, ଘରୁ ବାହାରୁ ନଥିଲେ, ସେହି ସମୟରୁ ଓଡ଼ିଶା ବିଜେପିକୁ ବଢାଇବା ପାଇଁ ସେ ଅଣ୍ଟା ଭିଡିଥିଲେ ।
 ତାଳଚେରରୁ ନିଜ ରାଜନୈତିକ ଜୀବନ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରି ପ୍ରଥମେ ମଣ୍ଡଳ ସଭାପତିର ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ନେଇ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲେ । ପରବର୍ତ୍ତୀ ସମୟରେ ବିଭିନ୍ନ ପଦ ପଦବୀରେ ଅବସ୍ଥାପିତ ହୋଇ ଓଡ଼ିଶାରେ ବିଜେପିକୁ ବଢାଇବାରେ ତାଙ୍କର ଯୋଗଦାନ ଥିଲା ଅତୁଳନୀୟ । 
 କେଉଁ ସମୟରେ, କେଉଁ ପରିସ୍ଥିତିରେ, କେଉଁ ଭଳି ଭାବେ ସେ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ କରୁଥିଲେ, ସେ ସମୟର ବ୍ୟକ୍ତିଙ୍କୁ ପଚାରିଲେ ହିଁ ବୁଝାପଡେ । 1988 ମସିହା ଫେବୃୟାରୀରେ ଯେତେବେଳେ ସେ ରାଜ୍ୟ ସଭାପତିର ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ନେଇ ସାରା ଓଡ଼ିଶା ବୁଲିବା ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲେ, ସେତେବେଳେ ଲୋକମାନେ ଭାବୁଥିଲେ ଏ ଲୋକ ପାଗଳ ହୋଇଯାଇ ନାହାନ୍ତି ତ, ବିଜେପି ଦଳ ଗଢିବା ପାଇଁ କେମିତି ଘରୁ ବାହାରିଛନ୍ତି ? ସେହି କଥାକୁ ଭ୍ରୁକ୍ଷେପ ନ କରି ସାରା ରାଜ୍ୟରେ ବିଜେପି ସଂଗଠନକୁ ବଢାଇବା ପାଇଁ ସେ ଲାଗି ପଡିଥିଲେ । ବିଜେପି କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକର୍ତ୍ତା ନିର୍ମାଣ କରିବା ସହ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକର୍ତ୍ତାଙ୍କୁ ବୁଝାଇବା ଏବଂ ବୁଥ, ମଣ୍ଡଳ ଓ ଜିଲ୍ଲା ସ୍ତରରେ ଦଳୀୟ ସାଂଗଠନିକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକୁ ସୃଦୃଢ କରିବା ଉପରେ ସେ ଯୋର୍ ଦେଉଥିଲେ । 
 1984 ମସିହାରେ ଦେବଗଡ ଲୋକସଭା ନିର୍ବାଚନମଣ୍ଡଳରୁ ପ୍ରଥମେ ସାଂସଦ ଭାବରେ ଲଢି 30,000 ଭୋଟ୍ ପାଇଥିଲେ । ମାତ୍ର ମନକୁ ଦୁର୍ବଳ ନ କରି ମନୋବଳକୁ ସୁଦୃଢ କରି ସେ ଆଗକୁ ବଢିବା ପାଇଁ ସ୍ଥିର କରିଥିଲେ । ତାଙ୍କର ଦକ୍ଷତା ଏବଂ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ କରିବାର ନିଷ୍ଠାକୁ ଦେଖି ପରବର୍ତ୍ତୀ ସମୟରେ ଆଉ ଦୁଇ ଦୁଇ ଥର ରାଜ୍ୟ ସଭାପତିର ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ମିଳିଥିଲା । 1998 ମସିହାରେ ଦେବଗଡ ନିର୍ବାଚନମଣ୍ଡଳୀରୁ ସାଂସଦ ଭାବରେ ନିର୍ବାଚିତ ହେବାର ସୌଭାଗ୍ୟ ଲାଭ କରି ଡାକ୍ତର ଦେବେନ୍ଦ୍ର ପ୍ରଧାନ ବାଜପେୟୀଙ୍କ ମନ୍ତ୍ରୀମଣ୍ଡଳରେ ସଡକ ଓ ପରିବହନ ରାଷ୍ଟ୍ର ମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଭାବରେ ଶପଥ ନେଇ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରିଥିଲେ । ପରବର୍ତ୍ତୀ ସମୟରେ ଦୁଇ ଦୁଇ ଥର ପୁନଃ ନିର୍ବାଚିତ ହୋଇ ସେ କୃଷି ରାଷ୍ଟ୍ର ମନ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଭାବରେ ମଧ୍ୟ ଦାୟିତ୍ୱ ତୁଲାଇଥିଲେ ।

ഇന്ത്യയുടെ സുരക്ഷിതത്വം എന്തുമാത്രം ബലമുള്ളതാണ് എന്ന് ഇപ്പോൾ മനസിലായില്ലേ ഒരുരാജ്യത്തെ നിയമം ആരാജ്യത്തുള്ള ജനങ്ങൾക്കു വേണ്ടിയുള്ളതാണ് ഇസ്ലാം ഇന്ത്യയുടെ താല്പര്യങ്ങളെ അംഗീകരിക്കുന്നില്ല എന്നതും സമൂഹീക വിരുദ്ധവും ഭരണഘടനയെ വെല്ലുവിളിക്കുന്നതും പോയി 2027 ൽ ഇന്ത്യയുടെ ഭരണം പിടിച്ചെടുക്കുമെന്ന് പ്രഖ്യാപിച്ചപ്പോളും ഓട്ട് നഷ്ടപ്പെടുമല്ലോ എന്നോർത്തു ഇവന്മാർക്ക് കുനിഞ്ഞു നിന്നുകൊടുത്തു ഇവിടുത്തെ മുഖ്യമന്ത്രിയും പ്രതിപക്ഷ നേതാവും അടക്കമുള്ള കൂട്ടിക്കൊടുപ്പ് രാഷ്ട്രീയക്കാർ..... എന്തു കൊണ്ടാണ് ഇസ്ലാം മതവിശ്വാസികളായ ഭൂരിഭാഗം ആളുകളും RSS നേയും BJP യെയും ശത്രുക്കളായി കാണുന്നത് എന്ന് ഇപ്പോൾ മനസ്സിലാക്കുന്നു ഇവരെ രാഷ്ട്രീയക്കാർ എന്ന് വിളിക്കാതിരിക്കുക മതതാത്പര്യങ്ങൾ സംരക്ഷിക്കാൻ രൂപം കൊടുത്ത മതഗുണ്ടകൾ അത്രയും മതി എന്തു പോക്രിത്തരവും ചെയ്തതിനു ശേഷം ന്യായികരിക്കാണും തെളിവുണ്ടാക്കാനും ഇടതു വലതു രാഷ്ട്രീയക്കാർ ഒപ്പം നിന്നുകൊടുത്തുകൊണ്ട് ഒരു ജനാധിപത്യ രാജ്യത്ത് മതതീവ്രവാദികളെ വളർത്തി വലുതാക്കിയ നേർകാഴ്ച ലോകത്ത് കേരളത്തിൽ മാത്രമേ കാണാൻ കഴിയു കുറ്റം ചെയ്യുന്നവരും കുറ്റകൃത്യത്തിന് കൂട്ടുനിൽക്കുന്നവരും ഇന്ത്യൻ നിയമപ്രകാരം കുറ്റവാളികളാണ് ഈ കൂട്ട് കുറ്റവാളികളെ സ്വാധീനിച്ചുകൊണ്ട് ഒരു രാഷ്ട്രത്തിന്റെ ഭരണം പിടിച്ചെടുക്കുക എന്നുള്ളത് മാരകമായ കുറ്റകൃത്യമായി കോടതികൾക്കുപോലും തോന്നുന്നില്ലേ എന്നതാണ് അത്‍ഭുതകരം ഇവിടെ വർഗീയകആലാപങ്ങളിൽ സാധാരണക്കാരായ ജനങ്ങളെ തള്ളിവിടുകയാണ് ഭരണകൂടം ചെയ്തു കൊണ്ടിരിക്കുന്നത് മാറാടുപോലുള്ള സംഭവങ്ങൾ അതിനു ഉദാഹരണങ്ങൾ അല്ലേ ഇസ്ലാം ഇന്ത്യയിൽ വിതറിയ സ്ലോ പോയിസൻ ആണ് എന്ന് ഓരോ ഹിന്ദുവുംക്രിസ്ത്യാനിയും അറിഞ്ഞു വെക്കുക ഈ മതരാഷ്ട്ര വാദികൾക്കു കുനിഞ്ഞു നിന്നുകൊടുക്കുന്ന ഒറ്റുകാരെ ഇനി വിശ്വസിക്കണമോ തീരുമാനിക്കുക അല്ലെങ്കിൽ സ്വന്തം രാജ്യത്ത് അടിമകളായി ജീവിക്കേണ്ടിവരും പാഴ്സികളുടെ രാജ്യമായ ഇറാനിൽ ക്രിസ്ത്യാനികൾ ജീവിക്കുന്നതുപോലെ ക്രിസ്തീയരാജ്യമായിരുന്ന ലേബനോനിൽ ക്രിസ്ത്യാനികൾ ജീവിക്കുന്നതുപോലെ പാക്കിസ്ഥാനിലും ബംഗ്ലാദേശിലും ഹിന്ദുക്കൾ ജീവിക്കുന്നതുപോലെ ഇവിടുത്തെ ഓരോ പൗരനും ആലോചിക്കൂ ഒരു പൊട്ടാൻ നിൽക്കുന്ന അണക്കെട്ടിന്റെ ചുവട്ടിൽ ജീവിക്കുന്ന ജനതയ്ക്കു പിന്നെ എന്തു സുരക്ഷയാണ് ഈ കൊള്ളക്കൂട്ടങ്ങൾ ഒരുക്കുന്നത്
